title: Assembly
description: Deep dive into x86/x64 assembly for game reverse engineering.

topics.title: Registers
topics.description: General purpose, segment, and flags registers.
topics.explanation:
x64 GPRs: RAX (accumulator), RBX (base), RCX (counter/arg1), RDX (data/arg2), RSI/RDI (source/dest index), RBP (frame pointer), RSP (stack pointer), R8-R15 (extended).

Special: RIP (instruction pointer), RFLAGS (ZF, CF, SF, OF). SIMD: XMM0-XMM15 for SSE/AVX.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Registers
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Registers in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Instructions Deep Dive
topics.explanation:
Categories: data transfer, arithmetic, logic, shift/rotate, control transfer, string ops, SIMD.

Prefixes modify behavior (REX for 64-bit, LOCK for atomics). ModR/M byte encodes operands.
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Instructions Deep Dive
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target

exercises: Write a summary explaining Instructions Deep Dive in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Stack Frames
topics.explanation:
Typical x64 prologue:
```asm
push rbp
mov rbp, rsp
sub rsp, 0x40
```
Local variables at `[rbp-0x10]`. With optimizations, RBP may be omitted — use RSP-relative addressing.

topics.title: Function Calls
topics.explanation: `call` pushes return address and jumps. `ret` pops into RIP. Tail call optimization reuses stack frame. Import thunks jump through IAT entries.

topics.title: Jump Instructions
topics.explanation: Unconditional: jmp. Conditional: je/jz, jne/jnz, jg, jl, ja, jb (based on flags). Loops: loop, jecxz (rare in modern code). Switch: jump table via `jmp [reg*8+disp]`.

topics.title: Memory Addressing Modes
topics.explanation: Forms: `[base + index*scale + displacement]`. RIP-relative common in x64: `[rip+0x1234]`. LEA computes addresses without memory access.

topics.title: SIMD Instructions
topics.description: SSE, AVX for vector math in games.
topics.explanation:
Games use SIMD for physics, graphics, audio. XMM registers hold 128-bit vectors. Instructions: movaps, addps, mulps, shufps.

Recognize SIMD-heavy functions by XMM register usage.
